categories.title: ANCIENT ROME
categories.theme: Questions directly about people, practices, or facts of Ancient Rome

clues.clue: This was the name given to someone who fought professionally in Roman arenas for the entertainment of crowds.
clues.fit_justification: Source question asks 'what was the name of someone who fought professionally in Roman arenas'; answer is 'A Gladiator', directly about Ancient Rome.

clues.clue: This Roman practice involved executing every tenth man of a mutinous cohort as a punishment.
clues.fit_justification: Source question references 'killing every tenth man in a mutinous Roman Cohort'; answer is 'Decimate', directly about Ancient Roman practice.

clues.clue: This Roman Emperor is notorious for appointing his horse to the Senate.
clues.fit_justification: Source question asks 'Which Roman Emperor made his horse a senator'; answer is 'Caligula', directly about a Roman Emperor.

clues.clue: This Roman gladiator led an unsuccessful slave revolt against Rome in 73 BC.
clues.fit_justification: Source question asks 'Which Roman Gladiator Lead An Unsuccessful Revolt Against Rome'; answer is 'Spartacus', directly about an Ancient Roman figure.

clues.clue: After his defeat at the Battle of Actium in 31 BC, this Roman general took his own life.
clues.fit_justification: Source question asks 'Which Roman killed himself after his defeat at Actium'; answer is 'Marc Antony', directly about an Ancient Roman historical figure.

finals.title: HISTORIC DECIPHERMENTS
finals.clue: Carved in 196 B.C. and discovered in 1799, this ancient stone tablet bearing the same text in three scripts allowed scholars to finally decode Egyptian hieroglyphics.
finals.fit_justification: Source question describes 'the ancient stone tablet that was carved in 196 B.C., and helped scholars decipher Egyptian hieroglyphs'; answer is 'The Rosetta Stone', a substantive and well-known historical artifact appropriate for a final clue.
